data.title: Verify Script - SegWit Node
data.comment:
• Still verify legacy P2PKH/P2SH exactly like pre-SegWit.
• The ‘amount’ field matters only when the spent output is a witness program.

data.title: Verify Script - pre SegWit nodes
data.comment: Pre-SegWit rules simulated—WITNESS + CLEANSTACK flags excluded, so a legacy node treats the OP_0 <20> output as anyone-can-spend and returns true.

data.content:
# Flow4 — Single-input P2WPKH → **two** P2WPKH outputs

Spend **one** P2WPKH UTXO into **two** P2WPKH outputs (payment + change). Keep it tight: **build two outputs → hashOutputs → preimage → witness → final tx & IDs**.

## 1) Outputs (lock ×2)

* Choose two amounts (leave fee headroom).
* Each `scriptPubKey` (P2WPKH): `00 14 <20B HASH160(recipient pubkey)>`.
* Serialize **both** outputs (amount\[8] + varlen + script) in the intended order.
  `hashOutputs = dSHA256( output_0 || output_1 )` ← order matters.

## 2) Tx-wide hashes (compute **once**)

* `hashPrevouts = dSHA256( txidLE||vout )`
* `hashSequence = dSHA256( sequence )`
* `hashOutputs`  = from **both** outputs (above)

## 3) BIP143 preimage (for this single input)
```version ||
hashPrevouts || hashSequence ||
outpoint (txidLE||vout) ||
scriptCode || amount || sequence ||
hashOutputs || locktime || 01000000
```

* `scriptCode` (for **prevout owner**): `0x19 76 a9 14 <20B HASH160> 88 ac`
* `amount` = **exact prevout value** (8-byte LE)

Sign `dSHA256(preimage)` with the prevout’s key; append `01` (SIGHASH\_ALL) to the DER signature.

## 4) Witness (per input, vin order)

`[ <DER_sig || 0x01>, <33B compressed pubkey> ]`
(Each item is prefixed by its **varint length**. One witness stack because there’s one input.)

## 5) Final serialization & IDs

```nVersion
marker=00 flag=01
vin: txidLE, vout, scriptSigLen=00, sequence
vout: output_0, output_1
witness: one stack for this input
nLockTime
```

* **TXID** = dSHA256 of the **base** tx (no marker/flag, no witness; scriptSig length `00`, bytes omitted).
* **wtxid** = dSHA256 of the **full** SegWit tx (includes marker/flag and witness).

## Quick checks (Flow4-specific)

* `hashOutputs` commits to **both** outputs in the same order you serialize them.
* `scriptCode` uses the **prevout’s** HASH160 (not either recipient).
* Witness stack item order/lengths are correct; stack aligns with **vin** index.
* Fee sanity: `Σ(in) − Σ(out) = fee` (use **vsize = ceil(weight/4)** for rate).
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Flow5 — 2× P2WPKH in → 1× P2WPKH out (consolidation)

Merge **two** P2WPKH UTXOs into **one** P2WPKH output. Keep it tight: **one output → shared tx-wide hashes → per-input preimages & signatures → two witnesses → final tx & IDs**.

## 1) Output (lock ×1)

* Build a single P2WPKH output: `00 14 <20B HASH160(recipient pubkey)>`.
* Serialize it once (amount\[8] + varlen + script).
* `hashOutputs = dSHA256( that one serialized output )`.

## 2) Tx-wide hashes (compute **once**)

* `hashPrevouts = dSHA256( outpoint0 || outpoint1 )` (vin order).
* `hashSequence = dSHA256( sequence0 || sequence1 )`.
* `hashOutputs` = from step 1 (reused for **both** inputs).

## 3) BIP143 preimages (one **per input**)

For each input i ∈ {0,1}:

```version ||
hashPrevouts || hashSequence ||
outpoint_i (txidLE||vout) ||
scriptCode_i || amount_i || sequence_i ||
hashOutputs || locktime || 01000000
```

* `scriptCode_i` (P2WPKH prevout owner): `0x19 76 a9 14 <20B HASH160(prevout_i pubkey)> 88 ac`.
* `amount_i` = exact value of that **prevout** (8-byte LE).

Sign `dSHA256(preimage_i)` with the key that controls **prevout i**; append `01` (SIGHASH\_ALL) to each DER signature.

## 4) Witnesses (two stacks, vin order)

* **Input 0 witness:** `[ <DER_sig0 || 0x01>, <33B pubkey0> ]`
* **Input 1 witness:** `[ <DER_sig1 || 0x01>, <33B pubkey1> ]`
  Each item is prefixed by its **varint length**. Order of witness stacks must match **vin** (0, then 1).

## 5) Final serialization & IDs

```nVersion
marker=00 flag=01
vin: [in0 scriptSigLen=00], [in1 scriptSigLen=00]
vout: [single P2WPKH output]
witness: [stack for in0], [stack for in1]
nLockTime
```

* **TXID** = dSHA256 of the **base tx** (no marker/flag, no witness; both inputs still have `scriptSigLen=00`, bytes omitted).
* **wtxid** = dSHA256 of the **full SegWit tx** (includes marker/flag + both witness stacks).

## Quick checks (Flow5-specific)

* Reuse the **same** `hashPrevouts`, `hashSequence`, and `hashOutputs` across **both** inputs (they’re per-transaction).
* `scriptCode_i` must match **each** prevout’s key hash (not the new recipient).
* Witness stacks are in **vin** order; item lengths (varints) are correct.
* TXIDs in vin are **little-endian**; explorers display big-endian.
* Fee sanity: verify `Σ(in) − Σ(out) = fee` using **vsize = ceil(weight/4)**.
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# SegWit: what changed and why it matters

A compact, practical map of Segregated Witness (BIP141/143) so you can build, sign, and verify SegWit v0 transactions with confidence.

## Why SegWit

* **Fixes malleability:** signatures move out of the TXID preimage → stable TXIDs (foundation for Lightning).
* **Speeds validation:** replaces legacy “rehash everything” with shared hashes, so each input signs **O(1)** unique data + **O(n)** shared data (no O(n²) blow-ups).
* **More throughput:** blocks have **4,000,000 weight units** (typical 1.6–2.0× gain vs legacy, workload-dependent).
* **Soft-fork upgrade:** old nodes stay consensus-compatible (they may not relay all witness spends by policy); upgraded nodes enforce witness rules.
* **Safer signing (HW-friendly):** the preimage **includes the spent amount**, preventing fee-skimming attacks.

## Transaction format (v0)

* **Marker/flag** right after `version` **when any input has witness**: `00 01`.
* **scriptSig**: empty for **native** P2WPKH/P2WSH inputs (note: P2SH-nested has a small redeemScript push here).
* **Witness section** at the end: one **witness stack per input**, in vin order.

### BIP143 digest (per input)

```hash = SHA256d(
  version ||
  hashPrevouts || hashSequence ||
  outpoint ||
  scriptCode || amount || sequence ||
  hashOutputs ||
  locktime || sighashType
)
```

**Field meanings**

* **hashPrevouts** = dSHA256 of all (txidLE||vout) — shared across inputs.
* **hashSequence** = dSHA256 of all sequences — shared across inputs.
* **outpoint** = this input’s (txidLE||vout).
* **scriptCode** (P2WPKH): `0x19 76 a9 14 <20B HASH160> 88 ac` (simulate legacy P2PKH).
* **amount** = **UTXO value being spent** (8 bytes).
* **hashOutputs** = dSHA256 of all outputs — shared across inputs.
* **sighashType**: usually `01000000` (SIGHASH\_ALL).

## Block rules & fees

* **Weight** = `base_bytes×4 + witness_bytes`.
* **vsize** = `ceil(weight / 4)`; fee rate uses **sat/vB**.
* **Base** = what legacy nodes see; **witness** = discounted data (4 witness bytes count as 1 vbyte).

## Addresses & scripts (v0)

* **P2WPKH (bech32 `bc1q…`/`tb1q…`)**
  `scriptPubKey`: `0 <20B>`; **witness**: `[ <DER-sig||0x01> , <33B pubkey> ]`.
* **P2WSH**
  `scriptPubKey`: `0 <32B>`; **witness**: `[ <stack items…> , <witnessScript> ]`.
* **Version byte**: `0` for v0; future v1–v16 may add new behaviors.

## TXID vs WTXID

* **TXID**: hash of the **base** tx (no marker/flag/witness) → stable vs signature tweaks.
* **wtxid**: hash of the **full** tx (includes witness).
* **Witness commitment**: miners commit to witness data via a merkle root in the coinbase output.

## Compatibility (soft-fork logic)

* Legacy nodes treat `0 <20B/32B>` as “anyone-can-spend.”
* Upgraded nodes **require** a valid witness and enforce **clean-stack** for the witness program.

## Gotchas to avoid

* Missing **amount** in the BIP143 preimage (mandatory).
* Wrong **scriptCode** (must match the **prevout’s** pubKeyHash, not your new output).
* Witness stacks out of order (must match **vin** order).
* Recomputing `hashPrevouts/Sequence/Outputs` per input (they’re per-tx).
* Using raw bytes for fees; always use **vsize** (sat/vB).
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Anyone-Can-Spend (ACS) demo: why old nodes accept it and new nodes don't

This mini-flow tries to spend the P2WPKH output from **Flow 1** **with an empty `scriptSig` and no witness** (and **no** SegWit marker/flag).

## What you’ll see

* **Legacy evaluation** (no `WITNESS`, no `CLEANSTACK`):
  `scriptSig || scriptPubKey = "" || (OP_0 <20B>)` just **pushes data** → top of stack is non-zero → **evaluates “true”** → appears spendable (“ACS”).
* **SegWit evaluation** (`WITNESS` + `CLEANSTACK`):
  `OP_0 <20B>` is a **v0 witness program**; must provide a witness stack `[ <sig||0x01> , <33B pubkey> ]` whose HASH160(pubkey) = that `<20B>`. With **no witness** → **invalid**.

## Build steps (concise)

1. **Select UTXO**: the P2WPKH from Flow 1 (`scriptPubKey = 0 <20B>`).
2. **ACS attempt** (legacy-style serialization):

   * **Do not include** SegWit marker/flag `00 01`.
   * Input → points to Flow 1’s UTXO; `scriptSigLen = 00`, `scriptSig = ""`.
   * No witness vector attached.
   * Output → anything (e.g., a P2PKH back to yourself).
3. **Verify twice**:

   * With legacy flags → **passes** (looks “anyone-can-spend”).
   * With SegWit flags (`WITNESS` + `CLEANSTACK`) and **spent\_amount provided** → **fails** (witness-program requires witness).

## Takeaways

* SegWit is a **soft fork**: old nodes remain compatible but don’t enforce witness rules.
* **Real P2WPKH spends** must carry the witness; that’s where signatures live under SegWit.
* **CLEANSTACK** is enforced for witness programs (no stray items).
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Lesson 8: Segregated Witness (SegWit) Transactions

- **Explain the SegWit soft-fork:** Demonstrate how a P2WPKH (Pay-to-Witness-Public-Key-Hash) output appears as "anyone-can-spend" to legacy nodes but is enforced by SegWit-aware nodes, which require a valid witness.
- **Build and sign a valid P2WPKH spend:** Construct the full BIP143 sighash preimage, which crucially includes the value of the output being spent to prevent fee attacks.
- **Create the Witness:** Move the signature and public key from the scriptSig into a separate witness structure appended to the end of the transaction.
- **Understand the new serialization format:** Assemble a transaction with the SegWit marker (`0x00`) and flag (`0x01`), an empty scriptSig for the witness input, and the witness data.
- **Fix transaction malleability:** Show that because the signature is no longer part of the data hashed to create the TXID, third-party signature malleability is eliminated, resulting in stable, predictable transaction IDs.
- **Handle complex transactions:** Construct and sign transactions with multiple SegWit inputs and outputs, correctly calculating the shared `hashPrevouts`, `hashSequence`, and `hashOutputs` fields for the sighash preimage.
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Flow3 — First valid SegWit spend (P2WPKH → P2WPKH)

Spend **one** P2WPKH UTXO into **one** P2WPKH output. Keep it laser-focused: **tx-wide hashes → BIP143 preimage → witness → TXID vs wtxid**.

## 1) Output (lock)

* Choose an amount (leave fee headroom).
* `scriptPubKey` (P2WPKH): `00 14 <20B HASH160(recipient pubkey)>` (bech32 encodes this).

## 2) Tx-wide hashes (compute **once**)

* `hashPrevouts = dSHA256( txidLE||vout )`
* `hashSequence = dSHA256( sequence )`
* `hashOutputs  = dSHA256( amount[8] || varlen || scriptPubKey )`

## 3) BIP143 preimage (for this single input)

```version ||
hashPrevouts || hashSequence ||
outpoint (txidLE||vout) ||
scriptCode || amount || sequence ||
hashOutputs || locktime || 01000000
```

* `scriptCode` (for **prevout owner**): `0x19 76 a9 14 <20B HASH160> 88 ac`
* `amount` = **exact prevout value** (8-byte LE)

Sign `dSHA256(preimage)` with the key that controls the **prevout**; append `01` (SIGHASH\_ALL) to the DER signature.

## 4) Witness (per input, in vin order)

`[ <DER_sig || 0x01>, <33B compressed pubkey> ]`
(Each item is prefixed by its **varint length**.)

## 5) Final serialization & IDs

```nVersion
marker=00 flag=01
vin: txidLE, vout, scriptSigLen=00, sequence
vout: amount + scriptPubKey
witness: one stack for this input
nLockTime
```

* **TXID** = dSHA256 of the **base tx**: same as above **but without marker/flag and without any witness section** (scriptSig remains length `00`, bytes omitted).
* **wtxid** = dSHA256 of the **full SegWit tx** (includes marker/flag and witness).

## Quick checks

* `amount` in preimage matches the **prevout** (byte-exact).
* `scriptCode` uses the **prevout’s** HASH160 (not the recipient’s).
* `txid` inside `vin` is **little-endian**; explorers display big-endian.
* Witness stack item order and **varint lengths** are correct.
* Fee math uses **vsize = ceil(weight/4)**; verify `Σ(in) − Σ(out) = fee`.
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
## Exercise 1 — SegWit Fee Comparison

**Goal:** Build identical transactions with **P2PKH** vs **P2WPKH** to compare fees.

**Setup**

* Same UTXO value: **200,000 sats**
* Two versions:

  * **A:** P2PKH → P2PKH (legacy)
  * **B:** P2WPKH → P2WPKH (native SegWit)
* Same output amount: **195,000 sats**
* Fee rate: **10 sat/vB**

**What to verify**

* Compute **weight** and **vsize**: `vsize = ceil(weight/4)`.
* Compare total fees at **10 sat/vB**; record **% savings** of SegWit vs legacy.
* Both script paths validate (legacy `scriptSig`, SegWit **witness**).
* Note: If your current flow only builds SegWit, build **B** and compute **A** analytically—or switch to your legacy flow to build **A**.

---

## Exercise 2 — Three-Way Split with SegWit

**Goal:** One **P2WPKH** input → **3× P2WPKH** outputs.

**Scenario (split dinner)**

* **Input:** 300,000 sats (P2WPKH)
* **Out 1:** Restaurant — 120,000 sats
* **Out 2:** Friend — 85,000 sats
* **Out 3:** Your change — *remainder minus fee*

**What to verify**

* `hashOutputs` covers **all three** outputs in the exact serialized order.
* Proper witness stack for the single SegWit input: `[sig||0x01, pubkey]`.
* Fee math uses SegWit discount (`weight → vsize → fee`).
* All three bech32 addresses are distinct.

---

## Exercise 3 — Mixed Input Consolidation

**Goal:** **P2PKH (legacy)** + **P2WPKH (native)** → single **P2WPKH**.

**Setup**

* **Input 1 (legacy P2PKH):** 100,000 sats
* **Input 2 (native P2WPKH):** 75,000 sats
* **Output:** P2WPKH — **173,000 sats** (adjust to hit your fee target)

**What to verify**

* Final tx includes **marker/flag `00 01`** (because at least one input is SegWit).
* **Input 1:** non-empty `scriptSig = [sig+01] [pubkey]`, **empty witness** (`00`).
* **Input 2:** `scriptSig = 00`, **witness** = `[sig+01, pubkey]`.
* Two different digests:

  * Legacy preimage for **Input 1** (no `amount`; `scriptCode` = prevout P2PKH template).
  * **BIP143** preimage for **Input 2** (includes `amount`; `scriptCode` = P2PKH template for the witness program’s keyhash).
* **Witness stacks follow input order** (vin 0, then vin 1).
* **TXID** (non-witness) vs **WTXID** (full) both computed; fee from `vsize`.

---

### Short Hint for Mixing Inputs (printable)

* If **any** input is SegWit: write **`00 01`** after `nVersion` and include a witness section.
* **Legacy P2PKH input:** `scriptSig = [sig+01] [pubkey]`, witness = `00`, legacy preimage (no `amount`).
* **Native P2WPKH input:** `scriptSig = 00`, witness = `[sig+01, pubkey]`, **BIP143** preimage (includes `amount`, `hashPrevouts/Sequence/Outputs`, `scriptCode` = P2PKH template of its keyhash).
* Witness stacks appear **in the same order as inputs**.
* **TXID** excludes witness; **WTXID** includes it.
* `weight = stripped*4 + witness`, `vsize = ceil(weight/4)`, `fee = vsize * feerate`.
data.title: Text Info Node
